extraction.one_line_summary: VINGEGAARD Tadej won Stage 7 with a 25-meter sprint on 24% gravel finish, taking back-to-back wins and extending yellow jersey lead to 35 seconds.
tactical_patterns: POGACAR attacked on 20% gravel section inside 1km but VINGEGAARD countered in final 25m on steeper 24% gradient.
UAE had systemic equipment failures with multiple bike swaps early stage, revealing pre-stage prep vulnerability for future mountain stages.
MARTINEZ Dani animated for INEOS but team still riding defensively despite needing to attack POGACAR/VINGEGAARD dominance.
Gravel finale at 24-26% gradient neutralized pure climbers, favoring explosive sprint power over sustained climbing rhythm.

rider_signals.POGACAR Tadej: negative: lost time bonuses by finishing second, now 35s down instead of narrowing gap through intermediate attacks
rider_signals.THOMAS Geraint: neutral: moved to third overall but losing further time gaps, INEOS still riding passively despite needing aggression
rider_signals.BARDET Romain: positive: DSM rider solid on steep finish after strong Giro, emerging as dark horse GC threat
rider_signals.QUINTANA Nairo: negative: surprisingly lost time on explosive gravel finish, revealing weakness on super-steep punchy gradients versus sustained climbs
rider_signals.LETAC Kamil: positive: went for stage win glory with late attack, showing aggressive tactical approach despite GC position
